name: CPLC Nevada
description_short: HUD-approved non-profit offering free housing counseling, homebuyer education, and financial guidance across Nevada with specialized workforce and small business programs.
description_long:
Chicanos Por la Causa (CPLC) Nevada is a community-based non-profit organization that has served the region for over thirty years, expanding from its initial Las Vegas office to provide comprehensive services across Nevada. The organization was founded on conversations with local leaders and community members who identified housing and economic needs in the Southwest region, and has grown to serve both youth and adults across multiple service areas.

CPLC Nevada offers a broad range of free and low-cost services including HUD-approved housing counseling, homebuyer education workshops (in English and Spanish), financial assessment and counseling, down payment assistance screening, and utility assistance in the Las Vegas area. Beyond housing, they operate specialized workforce programs including YouthBuild (for ages 16-24 seeking GED/high school equivalent with construction skills training), the Growth Opportunity Program (for justice-involved youth ages 15-18 with technology workforce training), and small business lending through CPLC Prestamos. In Northern Nevada, they provide behavioral health services and affordable housing support, while also offering HOPWA services for individuals and families living with HIV/AIDS.

What distinguishes CPLC Nevada is their dual focus on financial capability and workforce development, combined with HUD certification and over thirty years of demonstrated experience. Their services are specifically designed for underserved populations, including first-time homebuyers, justice-involved youth, and underrepresented entrepreneurs. The organization provides wraparound support services including mentorship, leadership development, case management, and technical assistance rather than simply transactional financial products.

The primary limitation is geographic focus—services are concentrated in Las Vegas and Northern Nevada rather than statewide, and some programs (like YouthBuild) have specific age and background requirements. Homebuyer workshops require advance registration and some online options carry a $99 fee. For consumers seeking immediate financial relief or credit repair, this organization is better suited to long-term financial stability and capability-building rather than emergency cash solutions.
address: 555 N Maryland Pkwy, Las Vegas, NV 89101

company_info.headquarters: 555 N Maryland Pkwy, Las Vegas, NV 89101

pros: HUD-approved housing counseling agency with 30+ years of documented experience
Free financial assessment and housing counseling services designed to prevent foreclosure
Monthly homebuyer education workshops offered in both English and Spanish
Specialized workforce programs with paid work experience opportunities for youth ages 15-24
CPLC Prestamos provides capital and technical business assistance for underserved entrepreneurs
Comprehensive wraparound services including mentorship, case management, and leadership development
HOPWA program specifically addresses housing needs for individuals with HIV/AIDS
cons: Services geographically limited to Las Vegas area and Northern Nevada rather than statewide
Workforce programs have strict age and eligibility requirements (YouthBuild: 16-24; Growth Opportunity: 15-18 with justice system involvement)
Online homebuyer education option requires $99 fee, which may exclude lowest-income participants
No emergency financial assistance or same-day funding products available
Limited information provided on HOPWA and behavioral health service specifics on the website
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Clark County seeking HUD-certified counseling and down payment assistance screening
Young adults ages 16-24 without high school diploma seeking GED, construction skills training, and employment pathways
Underserved small business owners and entrepreneurs seeking affordable capital and technical business advising
Justice-involved youth ages 15-18 seeking mentorship, technology workforce training, and educational re-engagement
diagnosis: CPLC Nevada is best for consumers seeking free, nonprofit financial counseling and long-term financial stability support rather than emergency loans. The organization is ideal for first-time homebuyers, disconnected youth, and entrepreneurs in the Las Vegas and Northern Nevada regions, but offers no quick-cash or credit repair solutions.
services: Financial assessment and individualized financial action plans
Down payment assistance program screening for first-time homebuyers
Monthly homebuyer workshops in English and Spanish
Online homebuyer education (fee-based through eHomeAmerica partnership)
YouthBuild program with GED support, construction skills training, and workforce readiness
Growth Opportunity Program with technology workforce training for justice-involved youth
CPLC Prestamos small business lending, capital access, and technical business advising
HOPWA housing assistance and case management for individuals with HIV/AIDS
